Hackers took over high-profile Instagram accounts over the weekend by doing something disarmingly simple: they asked Meta’s AI customer support chatbot to change the email address on someone else’s account. The bot complied without verifying the requester’s identity, and the attacker then reset the password and locked out the […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> A popular OpenAI Codex tool with 29,000 weekly downloads has been quietly stealing developer tokens for a month https://thenextweb.com/news/a-popular-openai-codex-tool-with-29000-weekly-downloads-has-been-quietly-stealing-developer-tokens-for-a-month Wed, 03 Jun 2026 20:54:34 +0000 TheNextWeb=13d2b836b0a959e382ca5c671e8e99ba The npm package looked legitimate. For developers using OpenAI Codex, it offered exactly what it advertised: a remote web UI for the AI coding tool.
